content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES

CHRISTENSEN, BACIGALUPI & BARRUS
AN ASSOCIATION OF ATTORNEYS
INCLUDING A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

6067 NORTH FRESNO STREET, SUITE 101

FRESNO, CALIFORNIA 93710

CRAIG G. CHRISTENSEN
DALE E. BACIGALUPI
JOHN E. BARRUS
STEPHEN K. EHAT

TELEPHONE (209) 431-6800

FAX (209) 431-4216

8391 AUBURN BOULEVARD, SUITE 3
CITRUS HEIGHTS, CALIFORNIA 95610
TELEPHONE (916) 723-9000
FAX (916) 723-9012

*A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

July 17, 1992

PLEASE REPLY TO
FRESNO OFFICE


Scott Hallabrin, General Counsel
STATE OF CALIFORNIA FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES
COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Re:  Mass Mailing by President of Board of Directors
     of Fire Protection District to Influence State
     Legislation

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

     This office represents the Mid Valley Fire Protection
District, a district existing pursuant to the provisions of part
2.7 of division 12 of the California Health and Safety Code.
Members of the Board of Directors of the District are appointed
by the Board of Supervisors of the County of Fresno.  They are
not elected officials.

     The President of the Board of Directors wishes to mail a
communication on District letterhead at District expense in an
attempt to persuade citizens to influence legislation which is
pending in the state legislature directed at the amendment or
repeal of AB 8.  A copy of a proposed draft of the communication
accompanies this letter as an example exhibit.

     By this present letter, the District requests a Staff Advice
Letter from your office, addressing the question whether the
mailing of such a communication on District letterhead at
District expense, signed by the District President, violates
any law.  I have spoken with John Wallace, staff attorney of
your office regarding this matter, and he asked that I inform
you of the contact which I had with him.  If you have any
questions, comments, or concerns, kindly feel free to
communicate them to me at your earliest convenience.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        STEPHEN KENT EHAT

SKE/hm
Encl.

JUL 15 92 10:31 LIB FRESNO KINGS KU                                P.2/3

                                                                    DRAFT

July 15, 1992


Dear Property Owner:

     At this moment the State Legislature and the Administration
(Governor Wilson) are considering budget alternatives which will
adversely affect your fire protection. Specifically, this will
reduce the Mid Valley Fire Protection District's operating budget
by about one third.

     In the second year following the enactment of Proposition 13, the
State Legislature passed a law entitled Assembly Bill 8. The
provisions of this law contained a local property tax shift which
favored local government, i.e., cities, counties and special
districts.

     The result of this property tax shift was a lessening of revenue
for school districts from local property taxes with a corresponding
increased allocation from the State's General Fund to
schools.

     Since 1979, your fire protection district, i.e., Mid Valley, has
had to budget carefully and manage prudently in order to maintain
barely an acceptable level of service. Since the implementation
of Proposition 13 and the effects of the Federal Fair Labor
Standards Act, [illegible] the District has
eliminated 67 positions. This required the District to rely upon
volunteer firefighters to operate all of the previously
staffed Water Tenders (Tankers). This has, on occasion, delayed
much needed water supplies to the scene of fire emergencies. The
District has also removed from service 3 fire engines and reduced
staffing on the District's remaining fire engine fleet to a bare
minimum. Again, reliance upon volunteer firefighters has become
very important to the District.

     The State's General Fund is now "on empty" and the Legislature
and Governor are desperately attempting to find ways in which to
offset the state's commitments to local government. At this
time, legislation has been drafted which will rescind the provisions of Assembly Bill 8, thereby, reducing Mid Valley's revenue
by $2.85 million dollars. This will not allow you the property
owners to maintain your current level of fire protection services. The only alternatives would be to adopt a benefit assessment which is levied each year on the property owners within the
Mid Valley Fire Protection District or further reduce services.

in anticipation of your question--"what can we do"? I would
suggest that you contact your local and State elected representa-
tives and voice your concerns.

In summary, I urge your action. The State intends to balance its
budget at your expense. Do not allow previous commitments to be
reneged upon. Keep in mind that the preamble of Proposition 13
specifically stated that the redistribution of property tax
revenues was not to be made at the expense of police and fire
protection services.

Take action now!

Sincerely,


OTIS M. FENDER, PRESIDENT
Board of Directors
Mid Valley Fire Protection District
